title: senecio pubigerus
intro: Senecio pubigerus, also known as the broom groundsel, is a plant species in the daisy family that is native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Senecio pubigerus are often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to aid in the relief of coughs and colds, as well as to support the immune system. However, it's essential to note that the plant contains toxic compounds called pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which can be hazardous in large quantities, so it's crucial to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
